title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: The strongest signal today is operational: frontier-risk reporting, provenance infrastructure, and new defenses for retrieval systems are moving from abstract safety discussion toward deployable controls. Fresh research also focuses on evaluation fidelity and policy-shaped training, suggesting the field is tightening how model behavior is measured, explained, and constrained.

items.title: METR publishes Frontier Risk Report for February–March 2026
items.why_it_matters: Regular frontier-risk reporting improves external visibility into model capability and deployment risks.

items.title: OpenAI details content provenance tools with Content Credentials, SynthID, and verification
items.why_it_matters: Provenance tooling can strengthen media authenticity checks and reduce confusion around AI-generated content.

items.title: BiRD proposes a bidirectional ranking defense for retrieval-augmented generation
items.why_it_matters: RAG-specific defenses matter as poisoning attacks increasingly target retrieval pipelines rather than base models.

items.title: Policy-aware rubric rewards aim to improve RLVR for multi-criteria model behavior
items.why_it_matters: Training rewards that encode policy constraints could make post-training behavior more governable and auditable.

items.title: ThoughtTrace introduces a dataset pairing user-AI chats with users’ self-reported thoughts
items.why_it_matters: Understanding hidden user intent could improve safety evaluations for manipulation, reliance, and harmful assistance.

items.title: Rethinking visual attribution for chest X-ray reasoning in large vision-language models
items.why_it_matters: It tests whether model explanations actually reflect evidence, a core issue for trustworthy high-stakes deployment.

items.title: CaMo introduces camera-motion-grounded evaluation for vision-language models
items.why_it_matters: Better evaluation can reveal when apparent spatial competence is benchmark gaming rather than real understanding.

items.title: MSAVBench targets more reliable evaluation of multi-shot audio-video generation
items.why_it_matters: As generative video systems scale, stronger benchmarks are needed to assess consistency and misuse-relevant failure modes.

items.title: Multi-axis Analysis of Image Manipulation Localization studies detection of edited images
items.why_it_matters: Improved manipulation localization supports forensic workflows against synthetic and edited media misinformation.

items.title: What Do Evolutionary Coding Agents Evolve? examines optimization dynamics in code-generating agents
items.why_it_matters: Mechanistic understanding of agentic coding systems could inform oversight of increasingly autonomous model behavior.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
